As Reported by K. Troy (RSP teacher):
While we were looking for an online book, Dean started making some noises, shaking his body, and rolling his
eyes back into his head. I asked him what was going on when he did that?
He told me he was possessed by the devil. Asked me if I know who the devil is. He said it is a
bad guy in the bible.
I asked if the devil was saying anything?
He said it was telling him to hurt people. I asked him if it was like last year when he was hearing
voices telling him to hurt people. He said yes. I asked if the voices were telling him how to hurt
people. He made a gun out of his hands and said, “pew, pew, pew, pew, pew, .” I asked if he
was making a gun and he said yes. I asked if it was telling him to do that to people in my room
(the room we were presently sitting). He said yes. I asked if the voices were telling him to hurt
people in Ms. Rossetti’s classroom? He said yes and named several boys from the classroom. I
asked if the voices were telling him to hurt Ms. Rossetti (no), Mr M (no), Me (no).
I asked if he had access to a gun. He asked what access meant. I told him access mean being
able to get a gun. I asked if his mom and dad had one. He said no. Then he shared his uncle
has one that he uses to get food. He then shared his uncle lives in Canada but that if he wanted
it, he could walk there.
I asked him if he has shared with Dr. Kate about hearing voices again. He said they only talk
about school stuff. I asked if he shared with his new Dr. about the voices and he said no. He
also said he did not share with his parents about the voices. I told him he could share with his
doctors so they can help quiet the voices.
Followed with a risk assessment and determined that he did not have access to a gun.  Contacted parents, SPED director, classroom teacher, counselor, admin.
Conducted Threat Assessment Protocol with counselor.  Threat determined to be non-substantive.

(the room we were presently sitting). He said yes. I asked if the voices were telling him to hurt
people in Ms. Rossetti’s classroom? He said yes and named several boys from the classroom. I
asked if the voices were telling him to hurt Ms. Rossetti (no), Mr M (no), Me (no).
I asked if he had access to a gun. He asked what access meant. I told him access mean being
able to get a gun. I asked if his mom and dad had one. He said no. Then he shared his uncle
has one that he uses to get food. He then shared his uncle lives in Canada but that if he wanted
it, he could walk there.
I asked him if he has shared with Dr. Kate about hearing voices again. He said they only talk
about school stuff. I asked if he shared with his new Dr. about the voices and he said no. He
also said he did not share with his parents about the voices. I told him he could share with his
doctors so they can help quiet the voices.
Followed with a risk assessment and determined that he did not have access to a gun.  Contacted parents, SPED director, classroom teacher, counselor, admin.
